actionable_feedback: Add a runnable reproducible_workbook artifact (reproduce_safety_trace.py or a Jupyter notebook) that contains the code/cells used to compute safety_trace_matrix.csv and remediation_backlog.md and produces final_decision_table.csv when run; include exact command(s) to run it in the repo root (e.g., python reproduce_safety_trace.py).

Include a distinct expert_memo.md (or label a section in remediation_backlog.md clearly as 'expert_memo') that documents sensitivity test details: which inputs were shifted, the exact code/commands used, and a one-line rationale for targeting observed_risk_score for +/-5% shifts.

Produce final_decision_table.csv (or final_decision_table.xlsx) that shows the per-option combination arithmetic and per-record post-remediation contributions so reviewers do not rely only on truncated markdown previews.

Resolve the documented ambiguity for O3 by explicitly implementing both plausible interpretations (A: reduce trace_gap contribution by 9%; B: improve normalized margin by 9%) and showing the resulting post-option margins and classifications for each interpretation; clearly state which interpretation will be used in recommendations and why.
Update remediation_backlog.md to reference the runnable script and final_decision_table.csv and add an explanation of the remediation math (e.g., formulas and a worked example for one option/set).
Add a short reproduce/validation section in the top-level README or remediation_backlog.md with the single command to re-generate all deliverables and the expected outputs/filenames.
